question_title: Which country will be ranked #1 in the World Happiness Report 2026?
summary: Finland has been ranked #1 in the World Happiness Report for 8 consecutive years (2018-2025), a streak verified via Wikipedia. The WHR methodology uses 3-year rolling averages of Gallup data (confirmed via Our World in Data), making sudden disruptions structurally very difficult. Multiple secondary sources already report Finland as #1 for WHR 2026, though the official report hasn't been published yet (expected ~March 2026). No methodology changes or disruptions to Finland's survey data have been reported. Denmark and Iceland appear in unverified 2026 projections as #2 and #3 respectively, making them the most plausible but still very unlikely challengers. The overwhelming evidence points to Finland retaining the #1 position.

factors.description: Finland has been #1 for 8 consecutive years (2018-2025), demonstrating extreme stability in WHR rankings. Verified via Wikipedia.

Multiple secondary sources (ibtimes, globalcitizensolutions, sevenseasworldwide) report Finland as #1 in WHR 2026 with a specific score of 7.736. Sources have minor inconsistencies suggesting projection rather than confirmed data, but are directionally informative.
WHR rankings use three-year averages of Gallup data (confirmed by Our World in Data), making year-to-year disruption structurally unlikely without sustained multi-year shifts.

Denmark and Iceland appear as #2 and #3 in unverified 2026 projections from secondary sources. As Nordic countries with similar social structures to Finland, they are plausible but unlikely challengers.
No reports of WHR methodology changes or major disruptions in Finland's Gallup survey scores that could alter the outcome.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Finland at 85%**

Finland has dominated the World Happiness Report rankings for 8 consecutive years (2018-2025). The report's three-year rolling average methodology structurally prevents sudden disruptions, and multiple secondary sources already project Finland as #1 for 2026. With no reported methodology changes or data disruptions, Finland is overwhelmingly likely to retain the top position.

| Option | Probability |
|--------|------------|
| Finland | 85% |
| Denmark | 8% |
| Iceland | 3% |
| Other | 4% |

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option corresponding to the country ranked first overall in the World Happiness Report 2026 global rankings, as published on the official World Happiness Report site before May 1, 2026. The primary source is the official World Happiness Report website at https://worldhappiness.report/.
fine_print: If the official site is unavailable at resolution time or does not provide the ranking table before May 1, 2026, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: Wikipedia overview page at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Happiness_Report
background_info:
The #1 ranking is a salient, widely cited headline metric reflecting comparative well-being, with implications for policy narratives and international benchmarking.
